unit-2 SB grammar box '▶️ More irregular verbs' teaches buy/bought (We bought a big box of chocolates); the verb and its past form are not word-bank entries
unit-2 SB grammar box '▶️ More irregular verbs' teaches buy/bought (We bought a big box of chocolates); the past form is not a word-bank entry
unit-2 SB grammar box '▶️ More irregular verbs' teaches know/knew (He knew it was wrong); the verb and its past form are not word-bank entries
unit-2 SB grammar box '▶️ More irregular verbs' teaches know/knew (He knew it was wrong); the past form is not a word-bank entry
unit-2 SB grammar box '▶️ More irregular verbs' teaches make/made (Jacob made a terrible mistake); the past form is not a word-bank entry (base 'make' is in-level)
unit-2 SB grammar box '▶️ More irregular verbs' teaches write/wrote (I wrote an email to the kids); the past form is not a word-bank entry (base 'write' is in-level)
unit-4 SB grammar box teaches the 'as ... as' / 'not as ... as' equality structure; 'as' is a structure token, not a standalone word-bank entry (only the phrase 'as soon as' is banked)
unit-4 SB grammar box 'Comparatives' Ausnahmen: good → better — irregular comparative, not a word-bank entry (base 'good' is in-level)
unit-4 SB grammar box 'Comparatives' Ausnahmen: bad → worse — irregular comparative, not a word-bank entry (base 'bad' is in-level)
unit-4 SB grammar box 'Superlatives' Ausnahmen: bad → the worst — irregular superlative, not a word-bank entry

standard irregular plural (-oes) of the item's own headword 'mosquito'; the regular inflection family only reaches 'mosquitos', so the correct spelling needs an item-scoped grant to stay a full-tier answer
unit-5 SB grammar box 'Directions (prepositions of place)' teaches Turn left/right; the bank holds the phrase 'to turn left' but not the token 'turn' (needed for 'turn right' too)
unit-5 SB grammar box teaches Turn left / take the first left; 'left' (direction) lives only inside bank phrases (to-turn-left), not as a standalone entry
unit-5 SB grammar box teaches Turn right / take the second right; 'right' (direction) lives only inside bank phrases (to-take-the-second-right), not as a standalone entry
unit-5 SB grammar box teaches Go straight ahead/on; 'straight' lives only inside bank phrases (to-go-straight-ahead/on), not as a standalone entry
unit-5 SB grammar box teaches Go straight ahead; 'ahead' lives only inside the bank phrase 'to go straight ahead', not as a standalone entry
unit-5 SB grammar box teaches 'on the corner of the next street' / 'round the corner'; the bank holds 'round-the-corner' but not the token 'corner'
unit-5 SB grammar box teaches 'beside the bank' as a preposition of place; not a word-bank entry (the bank has next-to/opposite)

unit-6 teaches position vocabulary left-hand / middle / right-hand; 'left' (the side) lives only inside the bank phrase 'left-hand', not as a standalone entry — needed to define right-hand by contrast

unit-6 teaches position vocabulary left-hand / middle / right-hand; 'right' (the side) lives only inside the bank phrase 'right-hand', not as a standalone entry — needed to define left-hand by contrast
unit-7 SB grammar box 'might / might not (Möglichkeit)' teaches the modal 'might'; not a word-bank entry (structure token, like should in u03)
unit-7 SB grammar box 'might / might not' teaches the negative contraction 'mightn't'; not a word-bank entry

unit-13 SB grammar box Will-future: will / won't / 'll express future predictions and decisions; the modal and its contractions are not word-bank entries

unit-13 Will-future contraction 'll: the cumulative-bank tokenizer strips the leading apostrophe, so the bare contraction surfaces as token 'll' (without apostrophe) — e.g. the transformation answer "'ll call". The pronoun forms (i'll, we'll, …) already match as whole tokens; this grants the standalone form.
